data.title: Vallourec S.A. reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Vallourec S.A. press release (OTCPK:VLOUF): Q2 Group EBITDA of €187 million with strong 22% margin
  • Revenue of $863M (-20.5% Y/Y).
  • Q3 2025 Group EBITDA expected to range between €195 million and €225 million
  • Confirm expected improvement in EBITDA in H2 2025 vs. H1 2025

data.title: NETSTREIT prices forward stock offering at $17.70 per share
data.description:
• NETSTREIT (NYSE:NTST) priced a public offering of 10.8M shares at $17.70 per share in connection with the forward sale agreements described below.
  • The company has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.62M shares of common stock.
  • The offering is expected to close on July 28, 2025.
  • The company initially will not receive any proceeds from the sale of shares of its common stock by the forward purchasers.
  • The stock price traded 1.5% lower on Thursday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Demand for repairs of U.S.-banned Nvidia AI chips soars in China - report
data.description:
Demand is surging in China for a business that, on paper, shouldn't exist: the repair of advanced Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) artificial intelligence chipsets banned by the U.S. from export to its trade and tech rival, Reuters reported on Friday.

According to two firms in Shenzhen, the country’s tech hub, roughly a dozen boutique companies now specialize in repairing Nvidia’s (NASDAQ:NVDA) H100 GPUs—along with A100 units and other high-end chips—that have somehow entered the country despite restrictions.

"There is really significant repair demand," said a co-owner of a firm that has been fixing Nvidia's gaming GPUs for 15 years and began working on AI chips in late 2024.

Business has been so good that the owners created a new company to handle those orders, which now repairs up to 500 Nvidia AI chips per month, the report said.

In related developments, Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) AI chips worth $1B were shipped to China in the three months after U.S. President Donald Trump tightened chip export controls, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.

data.title: Hexagon AB GAAP EPS of €0.09, revenue of €1.37B
data.description:
• Hexagon AB press release (OTCPK:HXGBF): Q2 GAAP EPS of €0.098.
  • Revenue of €1.37B (+1.5% Y/Y).
  • 3% organic revenue growth (using fixed exchange rates and a comparable group structure).
  • Adjusted gross margin of 67.0% (67.3).
  • Adjusted operating earnings (EBIT1) decreased by -10% to €360.6M.
  • Adjusted operating margin amounted to 26.3% (29.5).

data.title: Intel sees 15% workforce reduction as CEO Tan plans revival
Morgan Stanley issues first Panda bond by a US company - report
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) is said to have become the first American company to tap China’s panda bond market, with a 2 billion yuan ($279 million) offering.
  • Morgan Stanley BV, the firm’s investment management arm, priced its five-year notes at 1.98% on Thursday, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • That’s about five basis points higher than the average yield of similar yuan-denominated corporate notes.
  • The offering is the first Panda bond issued by a US firm, according to Bloomberg-compiled data.

data.title: ENI GAAP EPS of €0.16, revenue of €18.77B; raises FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ENI press release (NYSE:E): Q2 GAAP EPS of €0.16.
  • Revenue of €18.77B (-13.5% Y/Y).
  • Delivered €2.7 bln of pro forma adjusted EBIT, €1.13 bln of adjusted net profit and €2.8 bln of adjusted cash flow.
  • Outlook 2025: Eni is raising its FY ’25 CFFO outlook and confirming cash returns to shareholders, despite the headwinds of lower commodity prices and a weaker USD. Specifically, we are: • Raising the Group’s expected CFFO before working capital adjustments to circa €11.5 bln at the updated scenario1. This represents a €0.5 bln underlying improvement on the original Plan guidance. • Raising to around €3 bln from €2 bln the level of cash initiatives and other self-help measures aimed at mitigating the scenario effects. • Raising the FY projection of GGP’s proforma adjusted EBIT to around €1 bln (from a previous €0.8 bln) thanks to better than anticipated outcome from renegotiations and settlements, and portfolio optimizations.
  • In addition we: Confirm FY gross capex expected to be below €8.5 bln, down from an initial guidance of below €9 bln; net capex is seen below €6 bln from an initial guidance of €6.5-7 bln.
  • Continue to expect oil and gas production at 1.7 mln boe/d, in line with original assumptions. Q3 production is seen at between 1.7 and 1.72 mln boe/d.
  • Confirm Enilive and Plenitude outlook: o FY proforma adjusted EBITDA respectively of around €1 bln and above €1.1 bln; o End of year installed renewable capacity projected at more than 5.5 GW (Plenitude @100%); biorefinery capacity at 1.65 MTPA plus 1 MTPA under construction.
  • Robust balance sheet and leverage continue to be expected to be within the Plan stated range.
  • Leverage at year-end expected between 0.15 - 0.2 on a proforma basis.

data.title: Simpple regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• SIMPPLE (NASDAQ:SPPL) received notice from Nasdaq, notifying the company that it has regained compliance with the Nasdaq Capital Market’s minimum stockholders’ equity requirement and annual shareholder meeting requirement.
  • The company did not comply with the minimum stockholders’ equity of $2.5M. It executed a series of securities purchase agreements with investors to raise aggregate gross proceeds of $2M resulting from the sale of ~1.33M shares.

data.title: Absci stock dips after pricing $50M offering
data.description:
• Absci (NASDAQ:ABSI) has announced the pricing of a public offering of 16,670,000 shares of common stock at $3.00 per share.
  • The offering is expected to raise around $50 million in gross proceeds, before deducting underwriting discounts, commissions, and expenses.
  • The company has also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2,500,500 shares on the same terms. All shares are being offered by Absci.
  • Proceeds from the offering will support the advancement of Absci’s internal drug programs, continued development of its Integrated Drug Creation platform, and general corporate purposes, including working capital.
  • ABSI shares fell nearly 14% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Marex to acquire UK equity market maker Winterflood Securities
data.description:
Marex Group (NASDAQ:MRX) has agreed to acquire UK equity market maker Winterflood Securities (Winterflood) from Close Brothers for about £103.9 million in cash, which represents a premium of £15 million.

The acquisition is expected to enhance Marex’s existing UK cash equities business, consistent with its strategy to bring new clients and new capabilities onto its platform and diversify earnings.

"This deal is consistent with our strict financial criteria, and we see opportunities to materially improve Winterflood’s profitability and pay back its premium within two to three years," Ian Lowitt, Marex Group Chief Executive Officer said.

It is also expected to add a substantial distribution offering servicing the UK institutional community, in particular asset and wealth management companies, with the potential to deepen these relationships by offering access to a broader range of Marex’s products from across its platform.

The deal is subject to regulatory approval and is expected to close in early 2026.
